children.heading: Northeast Corridor Service Development Plan.—
children.label: a Northeast Corridor Service Development Plan.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Not later than , the Northeast Corridor Commission established under section 24905 (referred to in this section as the “Commission”) shall submit a service development plan to Congress.March 31, 2022

children.label: A
children.text: identify key state-of-good-repair, capacity expansion, and capital improvement projects planned for the Northeast Corridor;

children.label: B
children.text: provide a coordinated and consensus-based plan covering a 15-year period;

children.label: C
children.text: identify service objectives and the capital investments required to meet such objectives;

children.label: i
children.text: capital investment phasing;

children.label: ii
children.text: an evaluation of workforce needs; and

children.label: iii
children.text: strategies for managing resources and mitigating construction impacts on operations; and

children.label: E
children.text: include a financial strategy that identifies funding needs and potential funding sources.

children.heading: .—Updates
children.label: 3 .—Updates
children.text: The Commission shall update the service development plan not less frequently than once every 5 years.

children.heading: Northeast Corridor Capital Investment Plan.—
children.label: b Northeast Corridor Capital Investment Plan.—
A
children.text: develop an annual capital investment plan for the Northeast Corridor; and

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary of Transportation;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives.

children.label: A
children.text: reflect coordination across the entire Northeast Corridor;

children.label: B
children.text: integrate the individual capital plans developed by Amtrak, States, and commuter authorities in accordance with the cost allocation policy developed and approved under section 24905(c);

children.label: C
children.text: cover a period of 5 fiscal years, beginning with the fiscal year during which the plan is submitted;

children.label: i
children.text: the benefits and costs of capital investments in the plan;

children.label: ii
children.text: project and program readiness;

children.label: iii
children.text: the operational impacts; and

children.label: iv
children.text: Federal and non-Federal funding availability;

children.label: E
children.text: categorize capital projects and programs as primarily associated with 1 of the categories listed under section 24319(c)(2)(C);

children.label: F
children.text: identify capital projects and programs that are associated with more than 1 category described in subparagraph (E); and

children.label: i
children.text: funding sources and financing methods;

children.label: ii
children.text: the status of cost sharing agreements pursuant to the cost allocation policy developed under section 24905(c);

children.label: iii
children.text: the projects and programs that the Commission expects will receive Federal financial assistance; and

children.label: I
children.text: to receive the Federal financial assistance referred to in clause (iii); and

children.label: II
children.text: to implement each capital project.

children.heading: .—Review and coordination
children.label: 3 .—Review and coordination
children.text: The Commission shall require that the information described in paragraph (2) be submitted in a timely manner to allow for a reasonable period of review by, and coordination with, affected agencies before the Commission submits the capital investment plan pursuant to paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Failure To Develop a Capital Investment Plan
children.label: c .—Failure To Develop a Capital Investment Plan
children.text: If a capital investment plan has not been developed by the Commission for a given fiscal year, then the funds assigned to the Northeast Corridor account established under section 24317(b) for that fiscal year may be spent only on capital projects and programs contained in the Commission’s capital investment plan for the prior fiscal year.

children.heading: Northeast Corridor Capital Asset Management System.—
children.label: d Northeast Corridor Capital Asset Management System.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Amtrak and other infrastructure owners that provide or support intercity rail passenger transportation along the Northeast Corridor shall develop an asset management system and use and update such system, as necessary, to develop submissions to the Northeast Corridor capital investment plan described in subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: are consistent with the transit asset management system (as defined in section 5326(a)(3)); and

children.label: i
children.text: an inventory of all capital assets owned by the developer of the plan;

children.label: ii
children.text: an assessment of condition of such capital assets;

children.label: iii
children.text: a description of the resources and processes that will be necessary to bring or to maintain such capital assets in a state of good repair; and

children.label: iv
children.text: a description of changes in the condition of such capital assets since the submission of the prior version of the plan.

children.heading: .—Definition of Northeast Corridor
children.label: e .—Definition of Northeast Corridor
children.text: In this section, the term “Northeast Corridor” means the main line between Boston, Massachusetts, and the District of Columbia, and the Northeast Corridor branch lines connecting to Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, Springfield, Massachusetts, and Spuyten Duyvil, New York, including the facilities and services used to operate and maintain those lines.
